title: matricaria coronopifolia
intro: Matricaria coronopifolia, also known as false chamomile or maypop, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and bitter taste. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Matricaria coronopifolia can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
